words.def: a thin string of cotton, wool, silk, etc. used for sewing or making cloth
words.example: a needle and thread

words.def: the floor or ground at the bottom of a doorway, considered as the entrance to a building or room
words.example: He stepped across the threshold.

words.def: very excited and pleased
words.example: ‘Are you pleased?’ ‘I'm thrilled.’

words.def: to become, and continue to be, successful, strong, healthy, etc.
words.example: New businesses thrive in this area.

words.def: a regular rise and fall in the level of the sea, caused by the pull of the moon and sun; the flow of water that happens as the sea rises and falls
words.example: the ebb and flow of the tide

words.def: to hold or fix something more securely in position; to make something or become more difficult to move, open or separate
words.example: tighten something, to tighten a lid/screw/rope/knot

words.def: trees that are grown to be used in building or for making things
words.example: the timber industry

words.def: happening at exactly the right time
words.example: A nasty incident was prevented by the timely arrival of the police.

words.def: the dried leaves of the tobacco plant that are used for making cigarettes, smoking in a pipe, etc.
words.example: tobacco smoke

words.def: the quality of being willing to accept or tolerate somebody/something, especially opinions or behaviour that you may not agree with, or people who are not like you
words.example: She had no tolerance for jokes of any kind.

words.def: to allow somebody to do something that you do not agree with or like
words.example: tolerate something, Their relationship was tolerated but not encouraged.

words.def: money that you pay to use a particular road or bridge
words.example: motorway tolls

words.def: highest in position
words.example: He lives on the top floor.

words.def: the act of causing somebody severe pain in order to punish them or make them say or do something
words.example: Many of the refugees have suffered torture.

words.def: to throw something lightly or carelessly
words.example: toss something + adv./prep., I tossed the book aside and got up.

words.def: being the amount or number after everyone or everything is counted or added together
words.example: Their total cost was $18 000.

words.def: containing poison; poisonous
words.example: toxic chemicals/fumes/gases/substances

words.def: a mark, an object or a sign that shows that somebody/something existed or was present
words.example: It's exciting to discover traces of earlier civilizations.

words.def: a name, symbol or design that a company uses for its products and that cannot be used by anyone else
words.example: ‘Big Mac’ is McDonald's best-known trademark.

words.def: a long line or series of marks that is left by somebody/something
words.example: a trail of blood

words.def: a truck, or a container with wheels, that is pulled by another vehicle
words.example: a car towing a trailer with a boat on it

words.def: a piece of business that is done between people, especially an act of buying or selling
words.example: financial transactions between companies

words.def: a written or printed copy of words that have been spoken
words.example: a transcript of the interview

words.def: a complete change in somebody/something
words.example: The way in which we work has undergone a complete transformation in the past decade.

words.def: the process of being moved or carried from one place to another
words.example: The cost includes transit.

words.def: the act or process of passing something from one person, place or thing to another
words.example: the transmission of the disease

words.def: the quality of something, such as a situation or an argument, that makes it easy to understand
words.example: a need for greater transparency in legal documents

words.def: allowing you to see through it
words.example: The insect's wings are almost transparent.

words.def: a mental condition caused by severe shock, stress or fear, especially when the harmful effects last for a long time
words.example: the effects of trauma and stress on the body

words.def: a formal agreement between two or more countries
words.example: the Treaty of Rome

words.def: very great
words.example: a tremendous explosion

words.def: connected with a tribe or tribes
words.example: tribal art

words.def: a type of court with the authority to deal with a particular problem or disagreement
words.example: an international war crimes tribunal

words.def: an act, a statement or a gift that is intended to show your love or respect, especially for a dead person
words.example: At her funeral her oldest friend paid tribute to her life and work.

words.def: the part of a gun that you press in order to fire it
words.example: to pull/squeeze the trigger

words.def: a group of three people or things
words.example: A trio of English runners featured in the women’s 1 500 metres.

words.def: a great success, achievement or victory
words.example: one of the greatest triumphs of modern science

words.def: an object such as a silver cup that is given as a prize for winning a competition
words.example: a trophy cabinet

words.def: worried and anxious
words.example: She looked into his troubled face.

words.def: a person or an organization that has control of money or property that has been put into a trust for somebody
words.example: The bank will act as trustees for the estate until the child is 18.

words.def: the act of teaching something, especially to one person or to people in small groups
words.example: She received private tuition in French.

words.def: the number of people who attend a particular event
words.example: This year's festival attracted a record turnout.

words.def: the total amount of goods or services sold by a company during a particular period of time
words.example: an annual turnover of $75 million

words.def: the action of turning something with your hand, or of turning a part of your body
words.example: She gave the lid another twist and it came off.

words.def: a university or college student who is studying for their first degree
words.example: a first-year undergraduate

words.def: important in a situation but not always easily noticed or stated clearly
words.example: The underlying assumption is that the amount of money available is limited.

words.def: to make something, especially somebody’s confidence or authority, gradually weaker or less effective
words.example: Our confidence in the team has been seriously undermined by their recent defeats.

words.def: used to emphasize that something exists or is definitely true
words.example: There is undoubtedly a great deal of truth in what he says.

words.def: to join people, things, parts of a country, etc. together so that they form a single unit
words.example: The new leader hopes to unify the country.

words.def: that has never happened, been done or been known before
words.example: The situation is unprecedented in modern times.

words.def: to remove a cover or curtain from a painting, statue, etc. so that it can be seen in public for the first time
words.example: The Queen unveiled a plaque to mark the official opening of the hospital.

words.def: going to happen soon
words.example: the upcoming presidential election

words.def: the act of making a machine, computer system, etc. more powerful and efficient; the more powerful and efficient machine, computer system, etc.
words.example: instructions for installing an upgrade to the existing system

words.def: to support something that you think is right and make sure that it continues to exist
words.example: We have a duty to uphold the law.

words.def: a service provided for the public, for example an electricity, water or gas supply
words.example: a privatized electricity utility

words.def: to use something, especially for a practical purpose
words.example: The Romans were the first to utilize concrete as a building material.

words.def: completely
words.example: We're so utterly different from each other.

words.def: a space that is completely empty of all substances, including all air or other gas
words.example: a vacuum pump (= one that creates a vacuum)

words.def: not clear in a person’s mind
words.example: to have a vague impression/memory/recollection of something

words.def: the state of being legally or officially acceptable
words.example: The period of validity of the agreement has expired.

words.def: to disappear suddenly and/or in a way that you cannot explain
words.example: He turned around and vanished into the house.

words.def: often changing; likely to change
words.example: variable temperatures

words.def: of many different types
words.example: varied opinions

words.def: any of the tubes that carry blood from all parts of the body towards the heart
words.example: the jugular vein

words.def: a business project or activity, especially one that involves taking risks
words.example: A disastrous business venture lost him thousands of dollars.

words.def: relating to words
words.example: The job applicant must have good verbal skills.

words.def: an official judgement made in court or at an inquest (= an official investigation into somebody's death)
words.example: Has the jury reached a verdict?

words.def: to check that something is true or accurate
words.example: verify something, We have no way of verifying his story.

words.def: writing that is arranged in lines, often with a regular rhythm or pattern of rhyme
words.example: in verse, Most of the play is written in verse, but some of it is in prose.

words.def: used to show that two teams or sides are against each other
words.example: It is France versus Brazil in the final.

words.def: a tube that carries blood through the body of a person or an animal, or liquid through the parts of a plant
words.example: to burst/rupture a blood vessel

words.def: a person who has a lot of experience in a particular area or activity
words.example: the veteran American actor, Clint Eastwood

words.def: that can be done; that will be successful
words.example: a viable option/proposition

words.def: full of life and energy
words.example: a vibrant city

words.def: criminal activities that involve sex or drugs
words.example: At the door were two plain-clothes detectives from the vice squad.

words.def: violent and cruel
words.example: a vicious attack

words.def: a person who lives in a village
words.example: Some of the villagers have lived here all their lives.

words.def: to go against or refuse to obey a law, an agreement, etc.
words.example: to violate international law

words.def: the act of going against or refusing to obey a law, an agreement, etc.
words.example: They were in open violation of the treaty.

words.def: behaviour or attitudes that show high moral standards
words.example: He led a life of virtue.

words.def: connected with the voice
words.example: vocal music

words.def: to make a formal and serious promise to do something or a formal statement that is true
words.example: vow to do something, She vowed never to speak to him again.

words.def: the fact of being weak and easily hurt physically or emotionally
words.example: the vulnerability of newborn babies to disease

words.def: weak and easily hurt physically or emotionally
words.example: These offices are highly vulnerable to terrorist attack.

words.def: a separate room or area in a hospital for people with the same type of medical condition
words.example: a maternity/surgical/psychiatric/children’s ward

words.def: a building where large quantities of goods are stored, especially before they are sent to shops to be sold
words.example: Police are investigating a fire at a furniture warehouse.

words.def: the activity of fighting a war, especially using particular weapons or methods
words.example: air/naval/guerrilla warfare

words.def: a legal document that is signed by a judge and gives the police authority to do something
words.example: an arrest warrant

words.def: (especially in the past) a person who fights in a battle or war
words.example: a warrior nation (= whose people are skilled in fighting)

words.def: to make somebody/something less strong or powerful; to become less strong or powerful
words.example: The team has been weakened by injury.

words.def: to make cloth, a carpet, a basket, etc. by crossing threads or narrow pieces of material across, over and under each other by hand or on a machine called a loom
words.example: weave A from B, The baskets are woven from strips of willow.

words.def: a wild plant growing where it is not wanted, especially among crops or garden plants
words.example: The yard was overgrown with weeds.

words.def: in good health
words.example: I don't feel very well.

words.def: general health and happiness
words.example: emotional/physical/psychological well-being

words.def: not at all; not of any kind
words.example: They received no help whatever.

words.def: not at all; not of any kind
words.example: They received no help whatsoever.

words.def: by which; because of which
words.example: They have introduced a new system whereby all employees must undergo regular training.

words.def: during the time that something is happening; at the same time as something else is happening
words.example: In the UK it is illegal to drive whilst holding a mobile phone.

words.def: to hit a person or an animal hard with a whip, as a punishment or to make them go faster or work harder
words.example: He was taken back to the jail and soundly whipped.

words.def: completely
words.example: wholly inappropriate behaviour

words.def: to become wider; to make something wider
words.example: Her eyes widened in surprise.

words.def: a woman whose husband or wife has died and who has not married again
words.example: She gets a widow’s pension.

words.def: the measurement from one side of something to the other; how wide something is
words.example: The terrace runs the full width of the house.

words.def: the quality of being happy and ready to do something
words.example: Success in studying depends on a willingness to learn.

words.def: to rub something against a surface, in order to remove dirt or liquid from it; to rub a surface with a cloth, etc. in order to clean it
words.example: wipe something (on something), Please wipe your feet on the mat.

words.def: the ability to say or write things that are both clever and humorous
words.example: to have a quick/sharp/dry/ready wit

words.def: the act of taking an amount of money out of your bank account
words.example: You can make withdrawals of up to $250 a day.

words.def: a period of physical exercise that you do to keep fit
words.example: She does a 20-minute workout every morning.

words.def: the practice of showing respect for God or a god, by saying prayers, singing with others, etc.; a ceremony for this
words.example: an act/a place of worship

words.def: important, pleasant, interesting, etc.; worth spending time, money or effort on
words.example: It was in aid of a worthwhile cause (= a charity, etc.).

words.def: having the qualities that deserve somebody/something
words.example: Very few of his ideas are worthy of further attention.

words.def: to shout loudly, for example because you are angry, excited, frightened or in pain
words.example: yell (at somebody/something), He yelled at the other driver.

words.def: the total amount of crops, profits, etc. that are produced
words.example: a high crop yield

words.def: a young person or a child
words.example: The camp is for youngsters aged 8 to 14.
